title: Peach Farm is closing — but only temporarily
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Peach Farm in Boston's Chinatown is closing temporarily as its current owners retire after 31 years.
New owners, who previously ran East Ocean Seafood Restaurant, are taking over and plan to keep the Peach Farm name.
The Boston Licensing Board has approved the transfer of Peach Farm's food and beverage licenses to new owner Washan Ching.

title: Mass. U.S. attorney launches benefit, voter fraud task force
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Massachusetts U.S. Attorney Leah Foley announced a task force to combat benefit and voter fraud, focusing on programs like SNAP and MassHealth.
The new task force includes a public hotline for reporting suspected fraud and will be led by Assistant U.S. Attorneys Philip Mallard and Mark Grady.
The ACLU of Massachusetts criticized the inclusion of voter fraud in the task force, citing it as an overstated issue in the state.

title: United to offer couch-style economy seating — would you try it?
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: United Airlines will introduce 'Relax Row' couch-style seating in 2027, transforming three economy seats into a lie-flat space.
Relax Row will feature adjustable leg rests, custom-fitted mattress pads, blankets, pillows, and a travel kit for kids.
The seating option will be available on over 200 Boeing 787 and 777 aircraft by 2030, with up to 12 sections per plane.

title: Ad Club’s Women’s Leadership Forum stays alive amid DEI pushback
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: The Ad Club's Women's Leadership Forum drew over 1,000 participants despite challenges in corporate DEI support.
Held at Gillette Stadium, the forum featured speakers, panel discussions, and a business marketplace themed 'Unbound.'
CEO Barb Reilly emphasized the importance of maintaining DEI efforts amidst a shifting corporate landscape.

title: Juvenile injured in Allston shooting
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A juvenile was injured in a shooting on Thursday evening near 222 Brighton Ave in Allston, Boston.
The victim was found on Allston St with non-life-threatening injuries and was taken to a local hospital.
The shooter fled the scene, and no arrests have been made; police are investigating the incident.

title: He’s running his 11th Boston Marathon for Boston Children’s Hospital
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Steven Iannacone is running his 11th Boston Marathon to support Boston Children's Hospital.
Iannacone has dedicated his marathon runs to Jace Perkins for 10 years through the Miles for Miracles program.
Jace Perkins, who was once a 1.6-pound NICU baby, now thrives at almost 12 years old thanks to Boston Children's Hospital.

title: Did you follow the local news this week? Take our Greater Boston news quiz.
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: Boston.com offers a news quiz to test knowledge of Greater Boston's recent events.
Peter Chianca, Boston.com editor since 2019, oversees the general assignment section.
The quiz focuses on trending stories from the Greater Boston area.

title: Red Sox took advantage of MLB’s new automated strike challenge rules on Opening Day
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Red Sox defeated the Reds 3-0 on Opening Day, utilizing MLB's new Automated Ball-Strike Challenge System.
Catcher Carlos Narvaez and outfielder Roman Anthony successfully challenged umpire calls, overturning critical strike decisions.
Roman Anthony's challenge in the ninth inning helped secure a walk, leading to a key scoring opportunity for Boston.

title: In first Opening Day with Red Sox, Marcelo Mayer made quite the impression off the bench
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Marcelo Mayer went 2-for-2 as a pinch-hitter, contributing to Boston's 3-0 win over the Reds on Opening Day.
Despite not starting, Mayer's hits in the seventh and ninth innings scored runs that were crucial for the Red Sox victory.
Red Sox manager Alex Cora commended Mayer's performance but noted it doesn't guarantee him regular starts early in the season.

title: Lucky pup thanks NH firefighters for quick thinking that saved his life
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Ziggy the dog was injured on a solar light while his owners were on vacation.
Campton-Thornton firefighters applied a tourniquet, stopping severe bleeding and saving Ziggy's life.
Ziggy underwent surgery for a cut artery, nerves, and tendons, and is recovering well.
